https://hg.mozilla.org/mozilla-central/r.commit_message:
Bug 1760997 - Fix alignment of combined buttons in the customize panel overflow list. r=dao

Differential Revision: https://phabricator.services.mozilla.com/D142146

description.text: For bug 1698436, I added a specific CSS rule to fix the margins around the Profiler button (the `#profiler-button` element) when it's placed in the overflow menu (including while in the customize view). But it isn't clear why that one button should have been behaving differently in the first place, unlike the others fixed in that bug which are more obviously special; we should investigate and clean up this special case if possible.

ranked_scenarios.summary: Combined items (zoom controls, clipboard cut/copy/paste controls, profiler button) are misaligned inside the Overflow Menu

description.text:
Created attachment 9266449
screeshot issue.png

**Affected versions**
- 99.0a1 (2022-03-03) 

**Affected platforms**
- Windows 10
- macOS 11.0
- Ubuntu 20.04

**Steps to reproduce**
1. Go to Menu - More tools - Customise toolbar
2. Drag several items inside the Overflow Menu (make sure to include “Profiler” and “Edit Controls”)
3. Observe the Overflow Menu 

**Expected result**
- All items are aligned.

**Actual result:**
- “Profiler”, “Edit Controls”  are not aligned with the rest of the items.

**Regression range**
- Last good revision: 1ecdb4c33adf2064c910cbb15e66b6f066c9a5ce
- First bad revision: 41da8c4b62186e3c174278a8c97b375a00a23c2a
- Pushlog:
https://hg.mozilla.org/integration/autoland/pushloghtml?fromchange=1ecdb4c33adf2064c910cbb15e66b6f066c9a5ce&tochange=41da8c4b62186e3c174278a8c97b375a00a23c2a
Potential regressor: [1747437](https://bugzilla.mozilla.org/show_bug.cgi?id=1747437)

**Other notes**
- The issue is more visible inside the Customise page

+++ This bug was initially created as a clone of Bug #1747436 +++

This bug covers browser/themes/shared/customizableui/panelUI.inc.css .

I'm not sure if these rules are still needed, and if so (make sure to test the relevant widgets both pinned to the overflow panel and overflowing from the toolbar!) there's a way to simplify them (perhaps using a descendant selector + the panel's ID?) and/or use variables.
